verbGlossary.ORIGINATES IN: {source} is first created in {target}, typically as an editable draft before formal review begins.
verbGlossary.SUBMITTED AT: {source} is formally transmitted for owner or stakeholder review at {target}.
verbGlossary.ACCEPTED UPON: {source} is formally accepted and becomes the governing project timeline when {target} is reached.
verbGlossary.RETURNED TO: {source} is sent back to the contractor for correction after failing review at {target}.
verbGlossary.ROUTES THROUGH: {source} passes through {target} chain or workflow pattern as part of its review lifecycle.
verbGlossary.HELD DURING: {source} is paused at {target} while reviewers evaluate logic, durations, and resource allocations.
verbGlossary.FOLLOWS: {source} adheres to this standard {target} pattern from submission through disposition.
verbGlossary.ISSUED DURING: {source}schedule is distributed to field teams while the project is actively in {target}.
verbGlossary.ASSIGNED VIA: {source} deliverable is routed to a responsible party through {target}.
verbGlossary.BOUND BY: {source} is constrained by {target} component, such as a contractual submission deadline.
verbGlossary.TRIGGERED AT: {source}schedule is initiated when the project enters {target}, signaling the need for corrective action.
verbGlossary.AWAITS: {source} is queued and waiting for action at {target}; no progress until the reviewer acts.
verbGlossary.GRANTED UPON: {source} is formally awarded and the contract completion date is adjusted when {target} is reached.
verbGlossary.DENIED AT: {source} is refused at {target} and the original contract dates remain in force.
verbGlossary.COMPLETED AT: The milestone or activity reaches this terminal {target} when its scope of work is finished in the field.
verbGlossary.ESCALATES TO: {source} item automatically transitions to {target} when its required date passes without completion.
verbGlossary.ADVANCES TO: {source} transitions forward into {target} as field work progresses.
verbGlossary.IMPOSES: {source} forces this hold {target} on successor activities until the constraint date or condition is satisfied.
verbGlossary.RELEASED UPON: {source}constraint on successor activities is removed when {target} is reached, allowing work to proceed.
verbGlossary.AUTHORIZED UPON: {source}is formally approved and additional resources or overtime can be mobilized at {target}.
verbGlossary.LOGGED AT: {source} change is recorded at {target}, creating an audit trail of revisions to logic, dates, or durations.
verbGlossary.FINALIZED AT: {source}schedule is locked at this terminal {target}, becoming the permanent record of actual construction sequence.
verbGlossary.MEASURED BY: {source} metric is quantified using {target} performance measure.
verbGlossary.CORRELATED WITH: {source} KPI is cross-referenced against {target} metric to identify process-driven schedule impacts.
verbGlossary.TRACKED BY: {source} metric is monitored over time using {target} KPI.
